ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for kaya cake or pandan kaya cake exists; closest_parent 'cake food product' (FOODON_03301710) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for kaya cake; A00LC (Cakes and pastries) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for kaya cake or pandan kaya cake as a specific food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately one slice (120 g) of dripping kaya cake as served in a Singaporean café setting.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya (coconut jam) and sponge cake composite — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for sponge cake and coconut jam components, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for kaya-based confections
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Dripping Kaya Cake' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for yellow sponge cake (FDC ID 18133) with HPB Singapore data for kaya (coconut egg jam) spread, weighted approximately 70% sponge cake base and 30% kaya topping/drip by mass. Energy, macros, and secondary nutrients are derived from this composite estimation. Sodium reflects moderate addition from butter and kaya. Sugar is elevated due to kaya's high sucrose and coconut sugar content.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dripping Kaya Cake. GI estimated at 55 based on sponge cake GI values (range 46–66, mean ~55) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Kaya (coconut jam) contains sucrose and coconut sugar which moderately elevate GI; the fat and egg content of the sponge base attenuates glycemic response. GI of 55 is a conservative upper-bound estimate for this composite dessert. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 60.0) / 100 = 33.0, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 50.0 × 1.2 = 60.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~180 mg/100 g) and saturated fat from coconut milk and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a slice (approximately 60 g) to keep sodium intake below 110 mg per occasion, and should note that the saturated fat from coconut milk may contribute to vascular stiffness if consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~28 g/100 g) and elevated glycemic load per serving (~33)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Dripping Kaya Cake as an occasional indulgence only; the high sugar load from kaya and the glycemic load of approximately 33 per standard slice will produce a significant postprandial glucose excursion and should be accounted for within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~7.8 g/100 g) from coconut milk and butter, and cholesterol (~95 mg/100 g) from eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of Dripping Kaya Cake due to its saturated fat content derived from coconut milk and butter, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed regularly; occasional small portions are preferable to regular servings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dripping Kaya Cake is a Singaporean café-style dessert cake featuring a light, fluffy sponge cake base generously spread and drizzled with pandan kaya — a rich, fragrant coconut-egg jam flavoured with pandan leaf extract. The cake is characterised by its vivid green kaya custard drips cascading down the sides, giving it its 'dripping' name. Kaya (also spelled 'kaja') is a traditional Southeast Asian coconut jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaves, and is a beloved staple of Singaporean and Malaysian breakfast culture. In this dessert format, kaya is elevated into a luscious cake topping and filling. The texture combines the soft, airy crumb of a butter sponge with the smooth, creamy richness of kaya custard. The flavour profile is sweet, coconutty, and distinctly pandan-forward, with a subtle eggy richness. Nutritionally, a standard slice (approximately 120 g) provides around 408 kcal, with approximately 60 g of carbohydrate and 28 g of sugar per serving, making it a high-energy, high-sugar dessert. The saturated fat content (~9.4 g per slice) from coconut milk and butter warrants moderation for individuals managing cardiovascular risk. The glycemic load per serving is estimated at approximately 33, classifying it as a high glycemic load food per serving despite a moderate GI of 55. Clinically, this cake is best treated as an occasional treat for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian and generally halal-suitable. It is not suitable for those with gluten, egg, or dairy allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kaya cake?
What does kaya cake taste like?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Dripping Kaya Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy sponge cake data and HPB kaya data, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. GI is analogously estimated from sponge cake GI references in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on approximately 70% sponge cake base and 30% pandan kaya topping/drip by mass, derived from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 18133, yellow sponge cake) and HPB Singapore kaya data. No exact branded product nutritional panel was available from an approved source. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sponge cake entry or HPB kaya data for this food type. 3. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary composite source data used for this estimation. 4. GI is set to 55 (low category per ≤55 threshold) as an analogous estimate based on sponge cake GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008; this is the most defensible approved-source estimate available. 5. Price data from the input record (SGD 114.40) appears anomalous for a single slice of cake and likely represents a data entry error in the source field survey record; it has not been carried forward into the nutritional record. 6. The food is classified as Singaporean cuisine based on kaya being a quintessential Singaporean/Malaysian ingredient and the restaurant being a Singaporean café brand (Sinpopo Coffee). 7. Halal status is listed as likely true based on typical kaya cake ingredients (no pork or alcohol), but formal halal certification for Sinpopo Coffee has not been independently verified from an approved source.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake and HPB Singapore kaya data. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names. Populated ontology with defensible closest_parent codes. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg). Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Added zero-macro justification check — no core macros are zero so no justification phrase required. Set GI 55 with gi_category 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule.

Iteration 2: Verified all hard gates and Q1–Q8 criteria. Confirmed aliases consistency: all strings in aliases_by_language (椰浆蛋糕, 椰漿蛋糕, Kek Kaya, கயா கேக், காயா கேக், काया केक, and all English variants) appear verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed ontology match_type values are all within allowed set. Confirmed gi_category 'low' is correct for GI=55. Score confirmed 8/8. Final decision: PASS.
